STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""scripts/restart/open_github_issues.py
2	
3	PR-E: parse state/polaris_restart/issue_breakdown.md and open GitHub Issues
4	on aldrinor/polaris in the linear DAG order encoded by `Blocked by` /
5	`Blocks` fields.
6	
7	Per cleanup_audit.md PR-E scope + plan §6 canonical 10-PR table. Each
8	Issue body uses the §3 issue body template:
9	
10	    ## Scope
11	    ## Foundation refs
12	    ## Acceptance criteria
13	    ## Out of scope
14	    ## Adversarial inputs
15	    ## LOC estimate
16	    ## Per-Issue artifacts required at PR open
17	    ## Blocks
18	
19	Modes:
20	- --dry-run: parse + render bodies + print to stdout. NO gh API calls.
21	  Output captured to state/polaris_restart/pr_e_issues_dryrun.txt for
22	  Codex review.
23	- --apply: parse + render + call `gh issue create` for each Issue.
24	  Records {issue_id: github_issue_number} to
25	  state/polaris_restart/issue_github_map.json.
26	
27	Hard preconditions (Apply mode):
28	- gh CLI authenticated as aldrinor (verified by `gh auth status`).
29	- PR-D codex-required.yml workflow committed (verified by file existence).
30	- branch protection on aldrinor/polaris configured (USER ACTION 2 done).
31	
32	Usage:
33	    python scripts/restart/open_github_issues.py --dry-run
34	    python scripts/restart/open_github_issues.py --apply
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import argparse
40	import json
41	import os
42	import re
43	import subprocess
44	import sys
45	from pathlib import Path
46	
47	# PR-E iter 3 cosmetic fix during apply: Windows console (cp1252) chokes on
48	# Unicode chars (e.g. '→' U+2192) appearing in Issue titles. Force stdout to
49	# UTF-8 so print() works regardless of console codepage.
50	if sys.stdout.encoding and sys.stdout.encoding.lower() != "utf-8":
51	    try:
52	        sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
53	        sys.stderr.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
54	    except (AttributeError, OSError):
55	        pass
56	
57	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
58	ISSUE_BREAKDOWN = REPO_ROOT / "state" / "polaris_restart" / "issue_breakdown.md"
59	ISSUE_MAP = REPO_ROOT / "state" / "polaris_restart" / "issue_github_map.json"
60	DRYRUN_TRANSCRIPT = REPO_ROOT / "state" / "polaris_restart" / "pr_e_issues_dryrun.txt"
61	GH_REPO = "aldrinor/polaris"
62	
63	# PR-E iter 1 PRE-002 fix: implement §3a default Phase/Feature inheritance
64	# from issue prefix. Most Issues in issue_breakdown.md don't repeat
65	# Phase/Feature fields; they inherit from §3a defaults. Without this,
66	# 122 of 133 Issues would render as "(unspecified)".
67	#
68	# Mapping per state/polaris_restart/issue_breakdown.md §3a lines 114-125:
69	PHASE_BY_PREFIX: dict[str, str] = {
70	    "phase0": "0",
71	    "f1": "1",
72	    "f2": "1",
73	    "f3": "1",
74	    "f15": "1",
75	    "ecg": "1",
76	    "bug-079": "1",
77	    "bug-082": "1",
78	    "f4": "2A",
79	    "f5": "2A",
80	    "f7": "2A",
81	    "f8": "2A",
82	    "f9": "2A",
83	    "f6": "2B",
84	    "f10": "2B",
85	    "f13": "2B",
86	    "f14": "2B",
87	    "p2c": "2C polish",
88	    "f11": "3",
89	    "f12": "3",
90	    "bench": "3",
91	    "tpl": "3",
92	    "bug-084": "3",
93	    "sov": "4",
94	    "buf": "4.5",
95	    "hand": "5",
96	    "cj": "side-track",       # parallel — no phase number
97	    "anti": "side-track",
98	}
99	
100	FEATURE_BY_PREFIX: dict[str, str] = {
101	    "phase0": "infra",
102	    "f1": "F1",
103	    "f2": "F2",
104	    "f3": "F3",
105	    "f4": "F4",
106	    "f5": "F5",
107	    "f6": "F6",
108	    "f7": "F7",
109	    "f8": "F8",
110	    "f9": "F9",
111	    "f10": "F10",
112	    "f11": "F11",
113	    "f12": "F12",
114	    "f13": "F13",
115	    "f14": "F14",
116	    "f15": "F15",
117	    "ecg": "evidence-contract-gate",
118	    "p2c": "phase-2c-polish",
119	    "bench": "benchmark-templates",
120	    "tpl": "benchmark-templates",
121	    "sov": "sovereign-migration",
122	    "buf": "buffer",
123	    "hand": "handover",
124	    "cj": "crown-jewel-preservation",
125	    "anti": "anti-sycophancy-CI",
126	    "bug-079": "F1",            # 079 is intake/clinical_classifier
127	    "bug-082": "F15",           # 082 is audit-bundle health endpoint
128	    "bug-084": "F12-benchmark", # 084 is benchmark coverage scorer
129	}
130	
131	
132	def issue_id_prefix(issue_id: str) -> str:
133	    """Extract the prefix used for §3a defaults lookup.
134	
135	    Examples:
136	        I-phase0-003 -> "phase0"
137	        I-f1-001     -> "f1"
138	        I-bug-079    -> "bug-079" (the bug number IS the disambiguator)
139	        I-cj-001     -> "cj"
140	        I-anti-002   -> "anti"
141	    """
142	    # Strip "I-" prefix and trailing "-NNN" issue number
143	    m = re.match(r"^I-(.+?)-([0-9]{3})$", issue_id)
144	    if not m:
145	        return ""
146	    prefix = m.group(1)
147	    if prefix == "bug":
148	        return f"bug-{m.group(2)}"  # disambiguate by bug number
149	    return prefix
150	
151	
152	def default_phase(issue_id: str) -> str:
153	    return PHASE_BY_PREFIX.get(issue_id_prefix(issue_id), "")
154	
155	
156	def default_feature(issue_id: str) -> str:
157	    return FEATURE_BY_PREFIX.get(issue_id_prefix(issue_id), "")
158	
159	# PR-E iter 1 PRE-001 fix: bug-issue headers use `## §N I-bug-NNN — title`
160	# (not `### I-bug-NNN —`). Match BOTH ### (most issues) and ## §N (bug
161	# reissues) so we capture all 133 source Issues. Issue-id group 1 plus
162	# title group 2 unchanged.
163	ISSUE_HEADER_RE = re.compile(
164	    r"^(?:###|##\s+§[0-9]+)\s+(I-[a-z0-9]{2,8}-[0-9]{3})\s+[—-]\s+(.+?)(?:\s+\(reissued\))?$",
165	    re.M,
166	)
167	# Field pattern: matches `- **Name:** value` at line start; non-greedy on key
168	# so the same line with multiple `**Key:** value` pairs (e.g.
169	# `- **Phase:** 0 / **Feature:** infra`) splits correctly via repeated finditer.
170	FIELD_RE = re.compile(r"\*\*([A-Za-z][A-Za-z\s\-]*?):\*\*\s*([^\*\n]+?)(?=\s*\*\*[A-Za-z]|\s*$)")
171	
172	
173	def parse_issues(md_text: str) -> list[dict]:
174	    """Parse the issue_breakdown.md into a list of issue dicts.
175	
176	    Each issue dict contains: id, title, body_fields (Phase, Scope, etc.).
177	    Body composition is done separately in render_body().
178	
179	    Handles compound field lines like `- **Phase:** 0 / **Feature:** infra`
180	    by running FIELD_RE.finditer line-by-line so each `**Key:**` segment
181	    is treated as a separate field.
182	    """
183	    issues = []
184	    matches = list(ISSUE_HEADER_RE.finditer(md_text))
185	    # PR-E iter 3 PRE3-P2-002 fix: bound the LAST issue's block at the next
186	    # `## §N` section header (e.g. §31 doc-decommission, §32+ etc.) so any
187	    # tail bullets don't leak into the last Issue's parsed fields.
188	    SECTION_END_RE = re.compile(r"^##\s+§[0-9]+\s+", re.M)
189	    for i, match in enumerate(matches):
190	        issue_id = match.group(1)
191	        title_short = match.group(2).strip()
192	        start = match.end()
193	        if i + 1 < len(matches):
194	            end = matches[i + 1].start()
195	        else:
196	            # Last issue — search for the next ## §N header AFTER start
197	            tail = md_text[start:]
198	            section_match = SECTION_END_RE.search(tail)
199	            end = start + section_match.start() if section_match else len(md_text)
200	        block = md_text[start:end].strip()
201	        fields = {}
202	        # Walk line-by-line so multi-field lines are split correctly.
203	        for line in block.splitlines():
204	            line = line.strip()
205	            if not line.startswith("-"):
206	                continue
207	            # Strip leading "- " (bullet)
208	            content = line[1:].strip()
209	            for fm in FIELD_RE.finditer(content):
210	                key = fm.group(1).strip()
211	                value = fm.group(2).strip().rstrip("/").strip()
212	                if key and value:
213	                    fields[key] = value
214	        issues.append({
215	            "id": issue_id,
216	            "title_short": title_short,
217	            "fields": fields,
218	            "raw_block": block,
219	        })
220	    return issues
221	
222	
223	def render_body(issue: dict, issue_total: int = 133) -> str:
224	    """Render an Issue body using the §3 template.
225	
226	    Fills in §3 sections from parsed fields. Missing fields get explicit
227	    "(not specified in issue_breakdown.md)" markers so CI catches gaps.
228	
229	    issue_total is the count of all parsed Issues, used in the trailing
230	    footer line "one of N opened by PR-E" (PR-E iter 2 PRE2-P2-001 fix
231	    making the count dynamic instead of a stale literal).
232	    """
233	    ISSUE_TOTAL = issue_total  # noqa: N806 — local alias used inside f-string template
234	    f = issue["fields"]
235	    # PR-E iter 1 PRE-002 fix: §3a Phase/Feature inheritance — explicit
236	    # field wins, otherwise default by issue-id prefix.
237	    # PR-E iter 2 PRE2-P1-001 fix: when explicit Feature has parenthetical
238	    # annotation like `F1 (intake)` for bug Issues, prefer the canonical
239	    # default-by-prefix value to keep label consistent with `feature-f1`
240	    # not `feature-f1-intake`.
241	    explicit_feature = f.get("Feature", "").strip()
242	    default_ft = default_feature(issue["id"])
243	    if explicit_feature and "(" in explicit_feature and default_ft:
244	        # Parenthetical annotation present + a canonical default exists -> default wins
245	        feature = default_ft
246	    else:
247	        feature = explicit_feature or default_ft or "(unspecified)"
248	    phase = f.get("Phase", "").strip() or default_phase(issue["id"]) or "(unspecified)"
249	    scope = f.get("Scope", "(unspecified)")
250	    acceptance = f.get("Acceptance", "(unspecified)")
251	    # PR-E iter 2 PRE2-P1-002 fix: source-of-truth uses both `Foundation refs`
252	    # AND `Foundation` (4 Issues). Read both keys and prefer the longer.
253	    foundation = f.get("Foundation refs", "") or f.get("Foundation", "")
254	    out_of_scope = f.get("Out of scope", "(none specified)")
255	    adversarial = f.get("Adversarial inputs", "(none specified)")
256	    loc = f.get("LOC estimate", "(unspecified)")
257	    user_blocked = f.get("User-blocked", "NO")
258	    blocked_by = f.get("Blocked by", "(none)")
259	    blocks = f.get("Blocks", "(none)")
260	
261	    foundation_default_lines = [
262	        "- `state/polaris_restart/plan.md` §4 (master breakdown)",
263	        "- `polaris-controls/CHARTER.md` §1 + §3 + §4 + §7 (roles, LOC cap, immutable tests, visibility)",
264	        "- `polaris-controls/PLAN.md` (slice progression context)",
265	        f"- `docs/carney_delivery_plan_v6_2.md` Phase {phase} / Feature {feature}",
266	    ]
267	    if foundation:
268	        foundation_default_lines.append(f"- {foundation}  (Issue-specific)")
269	    foundation_block = "\n".join(foundation_default_lines)
270	
271	    body = f"""## Phase / Feature
272	
273	- **Phase:** {phase}
274	- **Feature:** {feature}
275	
276	## Scope
277	
278	{scope}
279	
280	## Foundation refs
281	
282	{foundation_block}
283	
284	## Acceptance criteria
285	
286	{acceptance}
287	
288	## Out of scope
289	
290	{out_of_scope}
291	
292	## Adversarial inputs
293	
294	{adversarial}
295	
296	## LOC estimate
297	
298	{loc} (≤200 per CHARTER §3)
299	
300	## Per-Issue artifacts required at PR open (CHARTER §7)
301	
302	- `.codex/{issue["id"]}/brief.md` (Claude-authored)
303	- `.codex/{issue["id"]}/codex_brief_verdict.txt` (Codex APPROVE)
304	- `.codex/{issue["id"]}/codex_diff.patch` (Claude-written diff with `# canonical-diff-sha256: <64-hex>` trailer)
305	- `.codex/{issue["id"]}/codex_diff_audit.txt` (Codex APPROVE on Red-Team checklist)
306	- `outputs/audits/{issue["id"]}/claude_audit.md` (Claude architect review)
307	
308	## Blocks
309	
310	- **Blocked by:** {blocked_by}
311	- **Blocks:** {blocks}
312	- **User-blocked:** {user_blocked}
313	
314	---
315	
316	_This Issue is one of {ISSUE_TOTAL} opened by PR-E from `state/polaris_restart/issue_breakdown.md` (Codex APPROVE iter 4). Branch convention: `bot/{issue["id"]}`. The codex-required.yml workflow (PR-D Codex APPROVE iter 7) gates merge on §3.0 5-artifact triple._
317	"""
318	    return body
319	
320	
321	def issue_label(issue: dict) -> list[str]:
322	    """Return GitHub labels for the Issue based on phase + feature.
323	
324	    Labels normalized to lowercase + hyphenated; rejects any label with
325	    invalid GH characters (slash, asterisk, etc. — defense against bad
326	    parses leaking into labels).
327	    """
328	    f = issue["fields"]
329	    labels = []
330	
331	    def safe_label(prefix: str, raw: str) -> str | None:
332	        v = raw.strip().lower().replace(" ", "-")
333	        # PR-E iter 3 PRE3-P2-003 fix: preserve `.` as `-` so Phase 4.5 -> phase-4-5
334	        # (was previously phase-45 because the dot was stripped by the alnum/hyphen
335	        # filter below, joining 4 and 5 with no separator).
336	        v = v.replace(".", "-")
337	        # Strip remaining non-alnum/hyphen chars
338	        v = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9_-]+", "", v)
339	        # Collapse repeated hyphens
340	        v = re.sub(r"-+", "-", v).strip("-")
341	        if not v:
342	            return None
343	        return f"{prefix}-{v}"
344	
345	    # PR-E iter 1 PRE-002 fix: same Phase/Feature inheritance as render_body
346	    # PR-E iter 2 PRE2-P1-001 fix: same parenthetical-annotation handling as
347	    # render_body so labels stay canonical (`feature-f1`, not `feature-f1-intake`).
348	    phase = f.get("Phase", "").strip() or default_phase(issue["id"])
349	    explicit_feature = f.get("Feature", "").strip()
350	    default_ft = default_feature(issue["id"])
351	    if explicit_feature and "(" in explicit_feature and default_ft:
352	        feature = default_ft
353	    else:
354	        feature = explicit_feature or default_ft
355	    if phase:
356	        lbl = safe_label("phase", phase)
357	        if lbl:
358	            labels.append(lbl)
359	    if feature:
360	        lbl = safe_label("feature", feature)
361	        if lbl:
362	            labels.append(lbl)
363	    # PR-E iter 1 PRE-003 fix: source values are `YES (account, billing, payment)`
364	    # etc. — the explanatory parenthetical follows YES. Match `YES` as a
365	    # whole-word prefix instead of exact equality.
366	    user_blocked_val = f.get("User-blocked", "").strip().upper()
367	    if user_blocked_val.startswith("YES"):
368	        labels.append("user-blocked")
369	    return labels
370	
371	
372	def gh_existing_labels() -> set[str]:
373	    """Return the set of label names that already exist on the repo.
374	
375	    PR-E iter 3 PRE3-P2-001 fix: FAIL CLOSED on list error. Earlier
376	    version returned an empty set on error, which combined with
377	    `gh label create --force` would treat existing labels as missing
378	    and overwrite their metadata. Now: any error raises RuntimeError
379	    so the apply-mode caller aborts before Issue creation.
380	    """
381	    try:
382	        result = subprocess.run(
383	            ["gh", "label", "list", "--repo", GH_REPO, "--limit", "200", "--json", "name"],
384	            capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
385	        )
386	        data = json.loads(result.stdout)
387	        return {entry["name"] for entry in data}
388	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as exc:
389	        raise RuntimeError(f"`gh label list` failed: {exc.stderr.strip()}") from exc
390	    except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError) as exc:
391	        raise RuntimeError(f"`gh label list` returned malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc
392	
393	
394	def gh_label_create(name: str, description: str = "Auto-created by PR-E") -> bool:
395	    """Create a single GH label, return True on success.
396	
397	    PR-E iter 3 PRE3-P2-001 fix: drop `--force` flag. We only call this
398	    function for labels NOT in the existing-set (preflight has confirmed
399	    they don't exist), so create-not-update is the right semantic. If
400	    the label somehow exists (race condition), the create returns
401	    "already exists" which is treated as success.
402	    """
403	    try:
404	        subprocess.run(
405	            ["gh", "label", "create", name, "--repo", GH_REPO, "--description", description],
406	            capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
407	        )
408	        return True
409	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as exc:
410	        # If error is "already exists" -> ok (idempotent). Else fail.
411	        if "already exists" in (exc.stderr or "").lower():
412	            return True
413	        print(f"ERROR: gh label create '{name}' failed: {exc.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
414	        return False
415	
416	
417	def ensure_labels_exist(needed_labels: set[str]) -> int:
418	    """PR-E iter 2 PRE2-P1-003 fix: label preflight.
419	
420	    Compute the union of labels needed by all 133 Issues, list existing
421	    labels via `gh label list`, create the missing ones via `gh label
422	    create`. Return count of labels created. If any creation fails,
423	    abort before any Issue is created (Apply mode).
424	    """
425	    existing = gh_existing_labels()
426	    missing = needed_labels - existing
427	    if not missing:
428	        print(f"Label preflight: all {len(needed_labels)} required labels already exist")
429	        return 0
430	    print(f"Label preflight: {len(missing)} missing labels to create: {sorted(missing)}")
431	    created = 0
432	    for name in sorted(missing):
433	        if gh_label_create(name):
434	            created += 1
435	        else:
436	            raise RuntimeError(f"Label preflight failed: could not create '{name}'")
437	    print(f"Label preflight: created {created} labels")
438	    return created
439	
440	
441	def gh_issue_create(issue: dict, body: str, labels: list[str]) -> int | None:
442	    """Call `gh issue create` and return the new issue number, or None on error."""
443	    title = f'{issue["id"]} — {issue["title_short"]}'
444	    cmd = [
445	        "gh", "issue", "create",
446	        "--repo", GH_REPO,
447	        "--title", title,
448	        "--body", body,
449	    ]
450	    for label in labels:
451	        cmd.extend(["--label", label])
452	    try:
453	        result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, check=True)
454	        # `gh issue create` returns the URL; extract the number.
455	        url = result.stdout.strip()
456	        m = re.search(r"/issues/(\d+)$", url)
457	        if m:
458	            return int(m.group(1))
459	        return None
460	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as exc:
461	        print(f"ERROR: gh issue create failed for {issue['id']}: {exc.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
462	        return None
463	
464	
465	def main():
466	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
467	    parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true", help="Print rendered bodies without creating Issues")
468	    parser.add_argument("--apply", action="store_true", help="Actually create GitHub Issues via gh CLI")
469	    args = parser.parse_args()
470	    if not (args.dry_run or args.apply):
471	        parser.error("must specify --dry-run or --apply")
472	    if args.dry_run and args.apply:
473	        parser.error("--dry-run and --apply are mutually exclusive")
474	
475	    md_text = ISSUE_BREAKDOWN.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
476	    issues = parse_issues(md_text)
477	    print(f"Parsed {len(issues)} Issues from {ISSUE_BREAKDOWN.name}")
478	
479	    if args.dry_run:
480	        with DRYRUN_TRANSCRIPT.open("w", encoding="utf-8", newline="\n") as fh:
481	            fh.write(f"# PR-E dry-run transcript\n\nParsed {len(issues)} Issues.\n\n")
482	            for issue in issues:
483	                body = render_body(issue, issue_total=len(issues))
484	                labels = issue_label(issue)
485	                fh.write(f"\n---\n\n## WOULD CREATE Issue {issue['id']}\n\n")  # dry-run mode
486	                fh.write(f"**Title:** `{issue['id']} — {issue['title_short']}`\n\n")
487	                fh.write(f"**Labels:** {labels}\n\n")
488	                fh.write(f"### Body\n\n{body}\n")
489	        print(f"DRY RUN — wrote transcript to {DRYRUN_TRANSCRIPT}")
490	        print(f"Re-run with --apply after Codex APPROVE on the transcript.")
491	        return 0
492	
493	    if args.apply:
494	        # Pre-flight gh auth check
495	        try:
496	            subprocess.run(["gh", "auth", "status", "--hostname", "github.com"], check=True, capture_output=True)
497	        except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
498	            print("ERROR: gh CLI not authenticated. Run `gh auth login`.", file=sys.stderr)
499	            return 1
500	
501	        # PR-E iter 2 PRE2-P1-003 fix: label preflight. Compute the union of
502	        # all labels needed by all 133 Issues and ensure each exists on the
503	        # repo BEFORE attempting any `gh issue create`. Without this, the
504	        # first Issue with a missing label fails the create call.
505	        all_labels = set()
506	        for issue in issues:
507	            for lbl in issue_label(issue):
508	                all_labels.add(lbl)
509	        try:
510	            ensure_labels_exist(all_labels)
511	        except RuntimeError as exc:
512	            print(f"ERROR: label preflight failed — {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
513	            return 2
514	
515	        existing_map = {}
516	        if ISSUE_MAP.exists():
517	            existing_map = json.loads(ISSUE_MAP.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
518	        created = 0
519	        skipped = 0
520	        failed = 0
521	        for issue in issues:
522	            if issue["id"] in existing_map:
523	                print(f"SKIP {issue['id']} (already created as #{existing_map[issue['id']]})")
524	                skipped += 1
525	                continue
526	            # PR-E iter 3 PRE2-P2-001-PARTIAL fix: pass dynamic count to
527	            # apply path too (was previously falling back to hard-coded 133).
528	            body = render_body(issue, issue_total=len(issues))
529	            labels = issue_label(issue)
530	            print(f"CREATE {issue['id']}: {issue['title_short']}")
531	            number = gh_issue_create(issue, body, labels)
532	            if number is None:
533	                failed += 1
534	                continue
535	            existing_map[issue["id"]] = number
536	            # Persist after each successful create so we can resume on failure
537	            ISSUE_MAP.write_text(json.dumps(existing_map, indent=2, sort_keys=True), encoding="utf-8")
538	            created += 1
539	            print(f"  -> #{number}")
540	
541	        print(f"\nDone: created={created}, skipped={skipped}, failed={failed}")
542	        if failed > 0:
543	            return 3
544	        return 0
545	
546	
547	if __name__ == "__main__":
548	    sys.exit(main())
549

2	"""
3	POLARIS Automated Deep Audit v2 CLI (FIX-222)

5	Standalone runner for the 10-dimension automated deep audit.
6	Can be used for regression testing against previous run results.
7	
8	Usage:
9	    python scripts/run_audit.py --result-file state/v3/result.json
10	    python scripts/run_audit.py --result-file state/v3/result.json --compare state/v3/prev_audit_v2.json

12	
13	import os
14	import sys
15	import json
16	import argparse
17	import logging
18	from datetime import datetime

20	
21	# Add project root to path
22	PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent.parent
23	sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT))
24	
25	logging.basicConfig(
26	    level=logging.INFO,
27	    format="%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(message)s",
28	)
29	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
30	
31	
32	def main():
33	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="POLARIS Automated Deep Audit v2")
34	    parser.add_argument("--result-file", type=str, required=True, help="Path to pipeline result JSON")
35	    parser.add_argument("--compare", type=str, help="Path to previous audit JSON for regression comparison")
36	    parser.add_argument("--output", type=str, help="Output path for audit JSON (default: auto-generated)")
37	    args = parser.parse_args()
38	
39	    result_file = Path(args.result_file)
40	    if not result_file.exists():
41	        logger.error(f"Result file not found: {result_file}")
42	        sys.exit(1)
43	
44	    logger.info(f"Running automated deep audit on: {result_file}")
45	
46	    try:
47	        from src.audit.automated_deep_audit import AutomatedDeepAudit
48	
49	        auditor = AutomatedDeepAudit()
50	        audit_result = auditor.audit_from_file(str(result_file))
51	
52	        if not audit_result:
53	            logger.error("Audit returned no results")
54	            sys.exit(1)
55	
56	        # Print summary
57	        logger.info("")
58	        logger.info("=" * 60)
59	        logger.info(f"DEEP AUDIT v2 TOTAL SCORE: {audit_result['total_score']:.1f}/100")
60	        logger.info("=" * 60)
61	
62	        for dim in audit_result.get("dimensions", []):
63	            bar = "#" * int(dim["score"])
64	            logger.info(
65	                f"  {dim['name']:<25} {dim['score']:5.1f}/10  "
66	                f"(weight {dim['weight']:.0%})  {bar}"
67	            )
68	
69	        logger.info(f"\n  Word count: {audit_result.get('metadata', {}).get('word_count', 'N/A')}")
70	        logger.info(f"  Sentence count: {audit_result.get('metadata', {}).get('sentence_count', 'N/A')}")
71	
72	        # Save output
73	        if args.output:
74	            output_path = Path(args.output)
75	        else:
76	            output_path = result_file.parent / f"{result_file.stem}_audit_v2.json"
77	
78	        with open(output_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
79	            json.dump(audit_result, f, indent=2, default=str)
80	        logger.info(f"\nAudit saved to: {output_path}")
81	
82	        # Regression comparison
83	        if args.compare:
84	            compare_path = Path(args.compare)
85	            if compare_path.exists():
86	                with open(compare_path) as f:
87	                    prev = json.load(f)
88	
89	                prev_score = prev.get("total_score", 0)
90	                curr_score = audit_result["total_score"]
91	                delta = curr_score - prev_score
92	
93	                logger.info("")

97	                logger.info(f"  Delta:    {delta:+.1f}")
98	
99	                if delta < -5:

101	                elif delta > 5:
102	                    logger.info("  IMPROVEMENT (>5 point gain)")
103	                else:
104	                    logger.info("  STABLE (within 5 points)")
105	
106	                # Per-dimension comparison
107	                prev_dims = {d["name"]: d["score"] for d in prev.get("dimensions", [])}
108	                for dim in audit_result.get("dimensions", []):
109	                    prev_dim_score = prev_dims.get(dim["name"], 0)

113	            else:
114	                logger.warning(f"Comparison file not found: {compare_path}")

116	        return 0

118	    except ImportError as e:

120	        sys.exit(1)
121	    except Exception as e:

123	        sys.exit(1)

125	
126	if __name__ == "__main__":
127	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""Slice 005 BEAT-BOTH benchmark CLI.
2	
3	Runs the live POLARIS chain against N benchmark questions, loads
4	manually-exported ChatGPT/Gemini DR outputs, scores all 3 systems on 7
5	dimensions, and emits scoreboard.json + summary.md + report.html.
6	
7	Usage:
8	    python scripts/run_benchmark.py \
9	        --config config/benchmark/clinical_n10.json \
10	        --polaris-url http://127.0.0.1:8000 \
11	        --chatgpt-dir external_outputs/chatgpt/ \
12	        --gemini-dir external_outputs/gemini/ \
13	        --output benchmark_results/clinical_n10_$(date +%Y%m%d)/
14	
15	Per `.codex/slices/slice_005/architecture_proposal.md` §"scripts/".
16	
17	Prerequisites:
18	- POLARIS FastAPI app running on --polaris-url with all 4 slices mounted
19	- SERPER_API_KEY + OPENROUTER_API_KEY set in env (so retrieval +
20	  generation work end-to-end against real backends)
21	- Manually-exported ChatGPT/Gemini outputs as .txt files named
22	  {question_id}.txt in --chatgpt-dir / --gemini-dir (missing files OK)
23	"""
24	
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import argparse
28	import logging
29	import sys
30	from pathlib import Path
31	
32	from polaris_graph.benchmark.beat_both_scorer import run_benchmark
33	from polaris_graph.benchmark.benchmark_config import load_config
34	from polaris_graph.benchmark.external_loader import load_external_outputs
35	from polaris_graph.benchmark.polaris_runner import run_polaris_against
36	from polaris_graph.benchmark.report_renderer import render_report
37	
38	
39	_LOG = logging.getLogger(__name__)
40	
41	
42	def parse_args(argv: list[str]) -> argparse.Namespace:
43	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(
44	        description="Run POLARIS BEAT-BOTH benchmark"
45	    )
46	    p.add_argument(
47	        "--config",
48	        required=True,
49	        type=Path,
50	        help="Path to benchmark config JSON (e.g. config/benchmark/clinical_n10.json)",
51	    )
52	    p.add_argument(
53	        "--polaris-url",
54	        default="http://127.0.0.1:8000",
55	        help="POLARIS FastAPI base URL (default: http://127.0.0.1:8000)",
56	    )
57	    p.add_argument(
58	        "--chatgpt-dir",
59	        type=Path,
60	        default=None,
61	        help="Directory of ChatGPT DR .txt outputs (optional)",
62	    )
63	    p.add_argument(
64	        "--gemini-dir",
65	        type=Path,
66	        default=None,
67	        help="Directory of Gemini DR .txt outputs (optional)",
68	    )
69	    p.add_argument(
70	        "--output",
71	        required=True,
72	        type=Path,
73	        help="Output directory for scoreboard.json + summary.md + report.html",
74	    )
75	    p.add_argument(
76	        "--skip-polaris",
77	        action="store_true",
78	        help=(
79	            "Skip the POLARIS run (use stub PolarisRunResult per question). "
80	            "Useful for re-scoring with fresh external outputs without "
81	            "re-running the chain."
82	        ),
83	    )
84	    p.add_argument(
85	        "-v", "--verbose",
86	        action="count", default=0,
87	        help="Increase log verbosity (-v=INFO, -vv=DEBUG)",
88	    )
89	    return p.parse_args(argv)
90	
91	
92	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
93	    args = parse_args(argv if argv is not None else sys.argv[1:])
94	    log_level = logging.WARNING
95	    if args.verbose >= 2:
96	        log_level = logging.DEBUG
97	    elif args.verbose >= 1:
98	        log_level = logging.INFO
99	    logging.basicConfig(
100	        level=log_level,
101	        format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s",
102	    )
103	
104	    _LOG.info("loading benchmark config from %s", args.config)
105	    config = load_config(args.config)
106	    _LOG.info(
107	        "config loaded: benchmark_id=%s, %d questions",
108	        config.benchmark_id, len(config.questions),
109	    )
110	
111	    if args.skip_polaris:
112	        _LOG.warning("--skip-polaris set; polaris_results will be empty")
113	        polaris_results = {}
114	    else:
115	        _LOG.info("running POLARIS chain against %s", args.polaris_url)
116	        polaris_results = run_polaris_against(
117	            config, args.polaris_url
118	        )
119	        succeeded = sum(1 for r in polaris_results.values() if r.succeeded())
120	        _LOG.info(
121	            "POLARIS: %d/%d questions succeeded",
122	            succeeded, len(polaris_results),
123	        )
124	
125	    _LOG.info("loading ChatGPT outputs from %s", args.chatgpt_dir)
126	    chatgpt_report = load_external_outputs(
127	        "chatgpt", args.chatgpt_dir, config.question_ids()
128	    )
129	    _LOG.info(
130	        "ChatGPT: %d/%d questions covered",
131	        len(chatgpt_report.loaded), len(config.question_ids()),
132	    )
133	
134	    _LOG.info("loading Gemini outputs from %s", args.gemini_dir)
135	    gemini_report = load_external_outputs(
136	        "gemini", args.gemini_dir, config.question_ids()
137	    )
138	    _LOG.info(
139	        "Gemini: %d/%d questions covered",
140	        len(gemini_report.loaded), len(config.question_ids()),
141	    )
142	
143	    _LOG.info("scoring %d questions across 7 dimensions", len(config.questions))
144	    scoreboard = run_benchmark(
145	        config=config,
146	        polaris_results=polaris_results,
147	        chatgpt_outputs=chatgpt_report.loaded,
148	        gemini_outputs=gemini_report.loaded,
149	    )
150	    _LOG.info(
151	        "scoreboard: polaris_wins=%d external_wins=%d ties=%d",
152	        scoreboard.polaris_wins,
153	        scoreboard.external_wins,
154	        scoreboard.ties,
155	    )
156	
157	    _LOG.info("rendering report to %s", args.output)
158	    files = render_report(scoreboard, args.output)
159	    for kind, path in files.items():
160	        _LOG.info("  wrote %s -> %s (%d bytes)", kind, path, path.stat().st_size)
161	
162	    print(f"\n=== POLARIS BEAT-BOTH benchmark complete ===")
163	    print(f"Benchmark: {scoreboard.benchmark_id}")
164	    print(f"Questions: {scoreboard.aggregate.n_questions}")
165	    print(
166	        f"POLARIS wins: {scoreboard.polaris_wins} | "
167	        f"External wins: {scoreboard.external_wins} | "
168	        f"Ties: {scoreboard.ties}"
169	    )
170	    print(f"Output: {args.output}")
171	    return 0
172	
173	
174	if __name__ == "__main__":
175	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""POLARIS live-run console (standalone, minimal, read-only).
2	
3	A tiny FastAPI app that makes a running ``run_honest_sweep_r3.py`` benchmark
4	fully visible to a BLIND (screen-reader) operator as ONE chronological,
5	ChatGPT-style scrolling log, plus a config panel over the PG_* env knobs.
6	
7	Design contract (LAW VII -- CLI isolation; LAW VI -- no hard-coding):
8	  * This module NEVER imports pipeline / faithfulness code. It only READS the
9	    observability files a run already writes, and READS/WRITES one ``.env``
10	    config file. It can never perturb a run or a verdict.
11	  * It is read-only on every run artifact. The only write is ``POST /api/config``
12	    editing a single line of the target ``.env``. The target ``.env`` is a
13	    SERVER-CONFIGURED path (``--env-file`` / ``PG_CONSOLE_ENV_FILE``, default
14	    ``<root>/.env`` else ``<repo>/.env``); requests can NEVER point it elsewhere.
15	    Keys are restricted to ``PG_[A-Z0-9_]+`` and values may not contain newlines,
16	    so the endpoint cannot smuggle extra keys or escape the intended file.
17	  * host / port / run-root / env-file come from argparse or env (PG_CONSOLE_HOST,
18	    PG_CONSOLE_PORT, PG_CONSOLE_ROOT, PG_CONSOLE_ENV_FILE) -- nothing is hard-coded.
19	
20	Observability files consumed (verified against the live emitters):
21	  * ``run_status.json``       heartbeat   -> phase / status / progress
22	      src/polaris_graph/telemetry/run_status_heartbeat.py:97-146
23	  * ``retrieval_trace.jsonl`` per-URL     -> query / fetch(kept|drop)
24	      src/polaris_graph/benchmark/pathB_capture.py:219-247
25	  * ``reasoning_trace.jsonl`` per-call    -> reasoning
26	      src/polaris_graph/generator/reasoning_trace.py:201-214
27	  * ``tool_trace.jsonl``      per-tool    -> tool
28	      src/polaris_graph/telemetry/tool_tracer.py:167-180
29	  * ``llm_io/<id>.json``      opt-in      -> reasoning (raw LLM I/O, AC3)
30	      src/polaris_graph/telemetry/llm_io_sink.py:34-72
31	  * ``report.md``             final       -> report + citation tokens
32	  * ``manifest.json``         terminal    -> status (+ release_disclosure.disclosed_gaps)
33	
34	The JSONL files are REWRITTEN in place (reasoning_trace ``"w"`` on every record;
35	retrieval_trace ``"w"`` flush at end-of-retrieval), so the tailer re-reads the
36	whole file each poll, tracks the emitted record COUNT, emits ``records[count:]``,
37	and resets on shrink. A poll landing mid-rewrite reads a truncated tail; the bad
38	line is skipped and picked up next poll. retrieval_trace records carry no
39	timestamp, so a single global ts-sorted order is impossible -- each file is
40	emitted in its own append order and the server stamps ``ts`` from the record's
41	own timestamp when present, else server-now. This is stated honestly in the UI.
42	"""
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import argparse
46	import asyncio
47	import json
48	import os
49	import re
50	import time
51	from pathlib import Path
52	from typing import Any, Iterable, Iterator
53	
54	from fastapi import FastAPI, Query, Request
55	from fastapi.responses import FileResponse, JSONResponse, StreamingResponse
56	
57	# -- constants (file names are pipeline-fixed artifact names, not tunables) -----
58	HOST_ENV = "PG_CONSOLE_HOST"
59	PORT_ENV = "PG_CONSOLE_PORT"
60	ROOT_ENV = "PG_CONSOLE_ROOT"
61	ENV_FILE_ENV = "PG_CONSOLE_ENV_FILE"  # server-configured target .env (NEVER request-controlled)
62	DEFAULT_HOST = "127.0.0.1"  # localhost-only: this process writes a .env from a request body
63	DEFAULT_PORT = 8787
64	
65	RUN_STATUS_FILE = "run_status.json"
66	RETRIEVAL_TRACE_FILE = "retrieval_trace.jsonl"
67	REASONING_TRACE_FILE = "reasoning_trace.jsonl"
68	TOOL_TRACE_FILE = "tool_trace.jsonl"
69	LLM_IO_DIR = "llm_io"
70	REPORT_FILE = "report.md"
71	MANIFEST_FILE = "manifest.json"

73	# The driver writes it as <root>/launch_<slug>.log (a SIBLING of the <domain>/<slug> run dir, i.e.

123	# -- helpers: safe IO --------------------------------------------------------
124	def _too_big(path: Path) -> bool:
125	    """True if path exceeds the read cap (DoS guard). Missing/unstatable -> not big."""
126	    try:
127	        return path.stat().st_size > _MAX_READ_BYTES
128	    except OSError:
129	        return False

132	def _read_json(path: Path) -> "dict[str, Any] | None":
133	    """Best-effort whole-file JSON read. Returns None on absence / mid-write garbage / oversize."""
134	    if _too_big(path):
135	        return None
136	    try:
137	        return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
138	    except Exception:
139	        return None

142	def _iter_jsonl(path: Path) -> Iterator[dict]:
143	    """Yield parsed JSON objects from a .jsonl, skipping blank / mid-rewrite-truncated lines.
144	
145	    The file is rewritten in place by the emitters, so a poll can land mid-write
146	    and read a truncated final line; that line fails json.loads and is skipped --
147	    it parses cleanly on the next poll once the rewrite completes. Files over the
148	    read cap are skipped entirely (DoS guard).
149	    """
150	    if _too_big(path):
151	        return
152	    try:
153	        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
154	    except Exception:
155	        return
156	    for line in text.splitlines():
157	        line = line.strip()
158	        if not line:
159	            continue
160	        try:
161	            yield json.loads(line)
162	        except Exception:
163	            continue
164

166	def _clamp(text: str) -> str:
167	    if len(text) > _MAX_TEXT:
168	        extra = len(text) - _MAX_TEXT
169	        return text[:_MAX_TEXT] + " ... (+" + str(extra) + " chars)"
170	    return text

173	def _resolve_run_dir(run: str) -> "Path | None":
174	    """Resolve a 'domain/slug' (or any sub-path) under the run root, refusing escapes."""
175	    base = _RUN_ROOT.resolve()
176	    candidate = (base / run).resolve()
177	    try:
178	        candidate.relative_to(base)
179	    except ValueError:
180	        return None  # path-traversal attempt
181	    return candidate if candidate.is_dir() else None

184	def _resolve_launch_log(run_dir: Path) -> "Path | None":
185	    """Resolve the per-run launch log for ``run_dir`` (GH #1258 PART 1), or None if absent.
186	
187	    Primary location: ``<root>/launch_<slug>.log`` — a SIBLING of the ``<domain>/<slug>`` run
188	    dir (``run_dir.parent.parent / ("launch_" + run_dir.name + ".log")``). Fallbacks: a log
189	    redirected INTO the run dir (``run.log`` / ``stdout.log``). Every candidate is derived from
190	    ``run_dir`` (already traversal-checked by ``_resolve_run_dir``) and confined under the run
191	    root, so this adds no new traversal surface. Returns the first EXISTING regular file.
192	    """
193	    candidates = [
194	        run_dir.parent.parent / ("launch_" + run_dir.name + ".log"),
195	    ]
196	    for name in LAUNCH_LOG_IN_RUN_FALLBACKS:
197	        candidates.append(run_dir / name)
198	    base = _RUN_ROOT.resolve()
199	    for cand in candidates:
200	        try:
201	            resolved = cand.resolve()
202	            resolved.relative_to(base)  # never tail anything outside the run root
203	        except (OSError, ValueError):
204	            continue
205	        if resolved.is_file():
206	            return resolved
207	    return None

210	def _tail_launch_log(path: Path, offset: int, carry: bytes):
211	    """Read launch-log bytes after ``offset`` and split into complete lines (GH #1258 PART 1).
212	
213	    Returns ``(lines, new_offset, new_carry)`` where:
214	      * ``lines``      = the COMPLETE lines (str) since ``offset``; the trailing partial line, if
215	                         any, is NOT emitted.
216	      * ``new_offset`` = the new byte position to resume from next poll.
217	      * ``new_carry``  = the buffered partial trailing line, held as RAW BYTES (no newline yet).
218	
219	    The carry is bytes, not text: a poll may end mid-multibyte-UTF-8-char (the read landed mid-char,
220	    or the per-poll byte cap split one), so we buffer the raw bytes and decode ONLY complete lines
221	    (those terminated by ``\\n``). Decoding the partial tail as text would freeze a half-char into a
222	    permanent replacement character; carrying bytes lets the next poll's bytes complete the char.
223	
224	    Rotation / truncation handling: if the file shrank below ``offset`` (rotated or rewritten),
225	    the offset and carry both reset to 0/b"" and we re-read from the start. Oversize files (over
226	    the DoS read cap) and IO errors yield no lines and leave the offset unchanged.
227	    """
228	    try:
229	        size = path.stat().st_size
230	    except OSError:
231	        return [], offset, carry
232	    # Rotation / truncation: file is now smaller than where we left off -> start over.
233	    if size < offset:
234	        offset = 0
235	        carry = b""
236	    if size == offset:
237	        return [], offset, carry
238	    # DoS guard: never read an unbounded backlog in a single poll. Cap the chunk to _MAX_READ_BYTES
239	    # from the current offset; the offset still advances so the next poll continues from there.
240	    end = min(size, offset + _MAX_READ_BYTES)
241	    try:
242	        with open(path, "rb") as fh:
243	            fh.seek(offset)
244	            chunk = fh.read(end - offset)
245	    except OSError:
246	        return [], offset, carry
247	    new_offset = offset + len(chunk)
248	    buf = carry + chunk
249	    parts = buf.split(b"\n")
250	    new_carry = parts.pop()  # trailing element: b"" if buf ended in \n, else the partial-line bytes
251	    lines = [p.rstrip(b"\r").decode("utf-8", errors="replace") for p in parts]
252	    return lines, new_offset, new_carry
253

256	def _list_runs(root: Path) -> list:
257	    """List run dirs (domain/slug, two levels deep) under root, newest first, with status.
258	
259	    The walk is bounded to ``_MAX_RUN_DIRS`` slug dirs so a pathological root with a
260	    huge number of subdirectories cannot pin CPU/IO (DoS guard).
261	    """
262	    runs: list = []
263	    if not root.is_dir():
264	        return runs
265	    walked = 0
266	    for domain_dir in root.iterdir():
267	        if walked >= _MAX_RUN_DIRS:
268	            break
269	        if not domain_dir.is_dir():
270	            continue
271	        for slug_dir in domain_dir.iterdir():
272	            if walked >= _MAX_RUN_DIRS:
273	                break
274	            if not slug_dir.is_dir():
275	                continue
276	            walked += 1
277	            rel = domain_dir.name + "/" + slug_dir.name
278	            manifest = _read_json(slug_dir / MANIFEST_FILE)
279	            status_doc = _read_json(slug_dir / RUN_STATUS_FILE)
280	            status = manifest.get("status") if isinstance(manifest, dict) else None
281	            stage = status_doc.get("stage") if isinstance(status_doc, dict) else None
282	            mtimes = []
283	            for name in (RUN_STATUS_FILE, MANIFEST_FILE, RETRIEVAL_TRACE_FILE):
284	                p = slug_dir / name
285	                if p.exists():
286	                    try:
287	                        mtimes.append(p.stat().st_mtime)
288	                    except OSError:
289	                        pass
290	            try:
291	                mtimes.append(slug_dir.stat().st_mtime)
292	            except OSError:
293	                pass
294	            runs.append(
295	                {
296	                    "run": rel,
297	                    "domain": domain_dir.name,
298	                    "slug": slug_dir.name,
299	                    "status": status,
300	                    "stage": stage,
301	                    "complete": bool(status),
302	                    "mtime": max(mtimes) if mtimes else 0.0,
303	                }
304	            )
305	    runs.sort(key=lambda r: r["mtime"], reverse=True)
306	    return runs

314	def _status_event(doc: dict) -> dict:
315	    stage = doc.get("stage")
316	    qi, qt = doc.get("query_index"), doc.get("query_total")
317	    parts = ["stage=" + str(stage)]
318	    if qi is not None and qt is not None:
319	        parts.append("query " + str(qi) + "/" + str(qt))
320	    if doc.get("slug"):
321	        parts.append("slug=" + str(doc.get("slug")))
322	    if doc.get("sources_kept") is not None:
323	        parts.append("sources_kept=" + str(doc.get("sources_kept")))
324	    if doc.get("sections_done") is not None or doc.get("sections_total") is not None:
325	        parts.append("sections=" + str(doc.get("sections_done")) + "/" + str(doc.get("sections_total")))
326	    if doc.get("claims_verified") is not None or doc.get("claims_total") is not None:
327	        parts.append("claims=" + str(doc.get("claims_verified")) + "/" + str(doc.get("claims_total")))
328	    if doc.get("running_cost_usd") is not None:
329	        parts.append("cost=$" + str(doc.get("running_cost_usd")) + "/" + str(doc.get("budget_cap_usd")))
330	    if doc.get("elapsed_s") is not None:
331	        parts.append("elapsed_s=" + str(doc.get("elapsed_s")))
332	    etype = "error" if (isinstance(stage, str) and stage == "error") else ("phase" if stage else "status")
333	    return _ev(etype, " ".join(parts), ts=doc.get("last_update_utc"))

390	def _report_events(report_path: Path) -> list:
391	    """One report event (the report landed) plus one citation event per unique [#ev:..] token."""
392	    out: list = []
393	    if _too_big(report_path):
394	        out.append(_ev("report", "report.md present but exceeds read cap; skipped"))
395	        return out
396	    try:
397	        text = report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
398	    except Exception:
399	        return out
400	    out.append(_ev("report", "report.md written (" + str(len(text)) + " chars)"))
401	    seen: set = set()
402	    for tok in _CITATION_RE.findall(text):
403	        if tok not in seen:
404	            seen.add(tok)
405	            out.append(_ev("citation", tok))
406	    return out

424	# -- SSE stream --------------------------------------------------------------
425	async def _event_stream(run_dir: Path):
426	    """Poll the run's observability files and yield each NEW event as an SSE frame."""
427	    counts = {RETRIEVAL_TRACE_FILE: 0, REASONING_TRACE_FILE: 0, TOOL_TRACE_FILE: 0}
428	    extractors = {
429	        RETRIEVAL_TRACE_FILE: _retrieval_event,
430	        REASONING_TRACE_FILE: _reasoning_event,
431	        TOOL_TRACE_FILE: _tool_event,
432	    }
433	    status_sig = None
434	    seen_llm: set = set()
435	    report_done = False
436	    manifest_done = False
437	    # GH #1258 PART 1: byte-offset state for the raw launch-log tailer. We read ONLY new bytes
438	    # each poll (seek(offset)+read), buffering a trailing partial line in `log_carry` until its
439	    # newline arrives, and reset on shrink/rotate. Kept in this closure exactly like `counts`.
440	    log_offset = 0
441	    log_carry = b""
442	
443	    yield _sse(_ev("status", "connected -- tailing " + run_dir.name))
444	
445	    while True:
446	        batch: list = []
447	
448	        # 1) heartbeat (emit only when its signature changes)
449	        doc = _read_json(run_dir / RUN_STATUS_FILE)
450	        if isinstance(doc, dict):
451	            sig = json.dumps(
452	                {k: doc.get(k) for k in ("stage", "query_index", "sources_kept",
453	                                         "sections_done", "claims_verified", "running_cost_usd")},
454	                sort_keys=True,
455	            )
456	            if sig != status_sig:
457	                status_sig = sig
458	                batch.append(_status_event(doc))
459	
460	        # 2) rewritten JSONL files: re-read whole, emit records[count:], reset on shrink
461	        for fname, extractor in extractors.items():
462	            records = list(_iter_jsonl(run_dir / fname))
463	            prev = counts[fname]
464	            if len(records) < prev:
465	                prev = 0
466	            for rec in records[prev:]:
467	                ev = extractor(rec)
468	                if ev is not None:
469	                    batch.append(ev)
470	            counts[fname] = len(records)
471	
472	        # 3) opt-in raw LLM I/O (one file per call, never rewritten)
473	        llm_dir = run_dir / LLM_IO_DIR
474	        if llm_dir.is_dir():
475	            try:
476	                files = sorted(llm_dir.glob("*.json"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime)
477	            except OSError:
478	                files = []
479	            # DoS guard: only the newest _MAX_LLM_IO_FILES files are read per poll.
480	            files = files[-_MAX_LLM_IO_FILES:]
481	            for f in files:
482	                if f.name in seen_llm:
483	                    continue
484	                d = _read_json(f)
485	                if isinstance(d, dict):
486	                    seen_llm.add(f.name)
487	                    batch.append(_llm_io_event(d))
488	
489	        # 4) final report.md (one-shot: report event + citation events)
490	        report_path = run_dir / REPORT_FILE
491	        if not report_done and report_path.exists():
492	            report_done = True
493	            batch.extend(_report_events(report_path))
494	
495	        # 5) terminal manifest (one-shot, keep the connection open afterwards)
496	        if not manifest_done:
497	            mdoc = _read_json(run_dir / MANIFEST_FILE)
498	            if isinstance(mdoc, dict) and mdoc.get("status"):
499	                manifest_done = True
500	                batch.append(_manifest_event(mdoc))
501	
502	        # 6) GH #1258 PART 1: incrementally tail the raw launch log (the per-run stdout). During
503	        # the long CPU embedding-rerank + agentic phases the pipeline writes progress ONLY here, so
504	        # without this the stream goes quiet for minutes even though work is happening. Emit each
505	        # NEW complete line as a type:"log" event (clamped); cap lines-per-poll so a burst can't
506	        # firehose the screen reader, coalescing the overflow into one notice.
507	        log_path = _resolve_launch_log(run_dir)
508	        if log_path is not None:
509	            new_lines, log_offset, log_carry = _tail_launch_log(log_path, log_offset, log_carry)
510	            if len(new_lines) > _MAX_LOG_LINES_PER_POLL:
511	                dropped = len(new_lines) - _MAX_LOG_LINES_PER_POLL
512	                new_lines = new_lines[:_MAX_LOG_LINES_PER_POLL]
513	                new_lines.append("(+" + str(dropped) + " more log lines this poll; raise "
514	                                 "PG_CONSOLE_MAX_LOG_LINES_PER_POLL to see them)")
515	            for line in new_lines:
516	                if line:  # skip blank lines (the screen reader gains nothing from them)
517	                    batch.append(_ev("log", line))
518	
519	        for ev in batch:
520	            yield _sse(ev)
521	
522	        await asyncio.sleep(POLL_INTERVAL_S)
523	
524	
525	def _sse(ev: dict) -> bytes:
526	    return ("data: " + json.dumps(ev, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n\n").encode("utf-8")

530	def _scan_pg_vars() -> dict:
531	    """Bounded walk of src/ + scripts/ for PG_* getenv/environ usage -> {name: default_or_None}."""
532	    found: dict = {}
533	    for top in _CONFIG_SCAN_DIRS:
534	        base = _REPO_ROOT / top
535	        if not base.is_dir():
536	            continue
537	        for path in base.rglob("*"):
538	            if path.suffix not in _CONFIG_SCAN_SUFFIXES or not path.is_file():
539	                continue
540	            try:
541	                if path.stat().st_size > _CONFIG_MAX_FILE_BYTES:
542	                    continue
543	                text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
544	            except OSError:
545	                continue
546	            for m in _PG_VAR_RE.finditer(text):
547	                name = m.group(1)
548	                default = m.group("def")
549	                if default is not None:
550	                    default = default.strip()
551	                    if (default.startswith(("'", '"')) and default.endswith(("'", '"'))) or default.isdigit():
552	                        default = default.strip("'\"")
553	                    else:
554	                        default = None
555	                if name not in found or (found.get(name) is None and default is not None):
556	                    found[name] = default
557	            for m in _PG_INDEX_RE.finditer(text):
558	                found.setdefault(m.group(1), None)
559	    return found

562	def _read_env_file(env_path: Path) -> dict:
563	    values: dict = {}
564	    if _too_big(env_path):
565	        return values
566	    try:
567	        text = env_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
568	    except Exception:
569	        return values
570	    for line in text.splitlines():
571	        s = line.strip()
572	        if not s or s.startswith("#") or "=" not in s:
573	            continue
574	        key, _, val = s.partition("=")
575	        values[key.strip()] = val.strip()
576	    return values
577	
578	
579	def _is_secret_key(name: str) -> bool:
580	    """True if a config key name looks secret-bearing (case-insensitive substring match)."""
581	    upper = name.upper()
582	    return any(marker in upper for marker in _SECRET_KEY_MARKERS)
583	
584	
585	def _mask_value(value: "str | None") -> "str | None":
586	    """Mask a secret-looking value: keep first2+last2 for long values, else a flat mask."""
587	    if value is None:
588	        return None
589	    if len(value) <= 6:
590	        return _SECRET_MASK
591	    return value[:2] + _SECRET_MASK + value[-2:]
592	
593	
594	def _set_env_var(env_path: Path, name: str, value: str) -> None:
595	    """Set/replace one ``NAME=value`` line, preserving every other line. Append if absent.
596	
597	    Refuses to read/rewrite an existing file over the read cap (DoS guard).
598	    """
599	    if _too_big(env_path):
600	        raise ValueError("target env file exceeds read cap")
601	    try:
602	        existing = env_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8") if env_path.exists() else ""
603	    except Exception:
604	        existing = ""
605	    lines = existing.splitlines()
606	    pat = re.compile(r"^\s*" + re.escape(name) + r"\s*=")
607	    replaced = False
608	    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
609	        if pat.match(line):
610	            lines[i] = name + "=" + value
611	            replaced = True
612	            break
613	    if not replaced:
614	        lines.append(name + "=" + value)
615	    env_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
616	    env_path.write_text("\n".join(lines) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
617	
618	
619	# -- routes ------------------------------------------------------------------
620	@app.get("/")
621	def index() -> FileResponse:
622	    return FileResponse(_HERE / "index.html", media_type="text/html")
623	
624	
625	@app.get("/api/runs")
626	def api_runs() -> JSONResponse:
627	    # Constrained to the server-configured _RUN_ROOT only. Any request-supplied
628	    # `root` is ignored so the endpoint cannot enumerate arbitrary directories.
629	    base = _RUN_ROOT.resolve()
630	    return JSONResponse({"root": str(base), "runs": _list_runs(base)})
631	
632	
633	@app.get("/stream")
634	async def stream(run: str = Query(...)):
635	    run_dir = _resolve_run_dir(run)
636	    if run_dir is None:
637	        return JSONResponse({"error": "run not found under root: " + run}, status_code=404)
638	    return StreamingResponse(
639	        _event_stream(run_dir),
640	        media_type="text/event-stream",
641	        headers={"Cache-Control": "no-cache", "X-Accel-Buffering": "no"},
642	    )
643	
644	
645	@app.get("/api/config")
646	def api_config() -> JSONResponse:
647	    # Reads ONLY the server-configured _ENV_FILE (no request-controlled path).
648	    # Secret-looking values are masked so a GET cannot disclose raw secrets.
649	    declared = _scan_pg_vars()
650	    current = _read_env_file(_ENV_FILE)
651	    rows = []
652	    for name in sorted(set(declared) | {k for k in current if k.startswith("PG_")}):
653	        raw = current.get(name)
654	        value = _mask_value(raw) if _is_secret_key(name) else raw
655	        rows.append(
656	            {
657	                "name": name,
658	                "default_if_any": declared.get(name),
659	                "current_value_from_env_file": value,
660	                "secret": _is_secret_key(name),
661	            }
662	        )
663	    return JSONResponse({"env_file": str(_ENV_FILE), "count": len(rows), "vars": rows})
664	
665	
666	@app.post("/api/config")
667	async def api_set_config(request: Request) -> JSONResponse:
668	    try:
669	        body = await request.json()
670	    except Exception:
671	        return JSONResponse({"error": "invalid JSON body"}, status_code=400)
672	    name = body.get("name")
673	    value = body.get("value")
674	    # Any `env_file` in the body is IGNORED: the write target is the single
675	    # server-configured _ENV_FILE only (no request-controlled paths).
676	    if not name or not isinstance(name, str):
677	        return JSONResponse({"error": "missing 'name'"}, status_code=400)
678	    if value is None:
679	        return JSONResponse({"error": "missing 'value'"}, status_code=400)
680	    if not isinstance(value, str):
681	        return JSONResponse({"error": "'value' must be a string"}, status_code=400)
682	    # Validate the key: ONLY PG_<UPPER/DIGIT/_>. fullmatch (not ^...$) so a
683	    # trailing newline like "PG_FOO\n" is rejected (\\$ would allow it).
684	    if not _PG_KEY_RE.fullmatch(name):
685	        return JSONResponse(
686	            {"error": "invalid 'name': must match PG_[A-Z0-9_]+"}, status_code=400
687	        )
688	    # Reject ANY line-break in the value: prevents key smuggling
689	    # (e.g. value="x\nPG_OTHER=y" injecting a second line into the .env).
690	    # NOTE: this guards against EVERY separator str.splitlines() breaks on --
691	    # not just \n/\r but also \v \f \x1c-\x1e \x85     -- because
692	    # _read_env_file / _set_env_var split the file with splitlines(); a narrower
693	    # check (only \n/\r) would let "x PG_OTHER=y" smuggle a second key.
694	    if "\n" in value or "\r" in value or len(value.splitlines()) > 1:
695	        return JSONResponse(
696	            {"error": "invalid 'value': must not contain a line break"}, status_code=400
697	        )
698	    try:
699	        _set_env_var(_ENV_FILE, name, value)
700	    except Exception as exc:
701	        return JSONResponse({"error": "write failed: " + str(exc)}, status_code=500)
702	    # Do NOT echo the raw value (it may be a secret). Return the key + the
703	    # resolved server path only.
704	    return JSONResponse({"ok": True, "name": name, "env_file": str(_ENV_FILE)})
705	
706	
707	# -- entrypoint --------------------------------------------------------------
708	def main() -> None:
709	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="POLARIS live-run console (read-only)")
710	    parser.add_argument("--host", default=os.environ.get(HOST_ENV, DEFAULT_HOST))
711	    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=int(os.environ.get(PORT_ENV, DEFAULT_PORT)))
712	    parser.add_argument(
713	        "--root",
714	        default=os.environ.get(ROOT_ENV, str(_REPO_ROOT / "outputs" / "honest_sweep_r3")),
715	        help="run output root; run dirs are <root>/<domain>/<slug>/",
716	    )
717	    parser.add_argument(
718	        "--env-file",
719	        default=os.environ.get(ENV_FILE_ENV),
720	        help="the ONE .env file /api/config reads and writes "
721	        "(default: <root>/.env, else <repo>/.env). Requests can never override it.",
722	    )
723	    args = parser.parse_args()
724	
725	    global _RUN_ROOT, _ENV_FILE
726	    _RUN_ROOT = Path(args.root).resolve()
727	    if args.env_file:
728	        _ENV_FILE = Path(args.env_file).resolve()
729	    else:
730	        candidate = _RUN_ROOT / ".env"
731	        _ENV_FILE = candidate.resolve() if candidate.exists() else (_REPO_ROOT / ".env").resolve()
732	
733	    import uvicorn
734	
735	    print(
736	        "[run_console] root=" + str(_RUN_ROOT)
737	        + "  env_file=" + str(_ENV_FILE)
738	        + "  http://" + args.host + ":" + str(args.port) + "/"
739	    )
740	    uvicorn.run(app, host=args.host, port=args.port, log_level="info")
741	
742	
743	if __name__ == "__main__":
744	    main()
